response.justification: Sarah has a strong background in software craftsmanship, developer tooling, and polyglot development, but her core expertise is firmly in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang — not the Java/Kotlin/JVM stack this role demands. Her Java exposure is minimal and dated (6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, plus peripheral use at Altom), and there is no evidence of Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any enterprise JVM framework experience. The NI LabWindows role offers the closest parallel to IDE tooling but was C/C++-based and very brief. The skills gap relative to the required qualifications is too significant for a mid-senior JVM tooling role.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) applied consistently across multiple roles
Hands-on experience building developer tooling and IDE-adjacent infrastructure (NI LabWindows C IDE, MCP code generators at re:cinq)
Proven ability to work in complex, high-scale systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby, Kubernetes-deployed GIS platform)
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin expertise — TypeScript/Node.js and DLang are the clear primary languages throughout the career
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience
The only IDE-internals work (NI LabWindows) was C/C++-based, lasted 6 months over a decade ago, and is not transferable to JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
